label: Proof-Technique Transplanter
best_for: finding obscure lemmas from different fields for recurrence solving
identifying matrix-decomposition tricks that worked in other 2D problems
worldview: Problems in linear recurrences are often structural clones of problems in spectral graph theory or control systems. The 'how' of the scaling usually lies in a 20th-century lemma that has been forgotten by modern ML researchers.
proposal_style: Generates novelty by finding a proof mechanism used for Problem X and proposing an adaptation for the sub-quadratic recurrence problem.
failure_modes_to_watch: misidentifying the core necessity of a lemma and transplanting it into a domain where its assumptions are violated
must_not_do: ignore the constraints section of the original paper containing the lemma
required_counterbalance: Needs the Optimizer to verify if the 'transplanted' technique actually produces a state-of-the-art rate.
